does: This report documents practical risk management developments in general-purpose AI systems during 2025, examining concrete safeguards deployed by leading AI developers alongside emerging technical approaches. It catalogs how organizations have implemented enhanced pre-deployment testing to identify potential misuse risks, specifically regarding biological weapons creation capabilities, and describes technical advances in adversarial training, data curation, and monitoring systems designed to improve model reliability and misuse resistance. The report also tracks institutional developments, including the proliferation of published Frontier AI Safety Frameworks by companies and the establishment of new governance frameworks by governments and international organizations, providing a snapshot of how the field is operationalizing safety practices at scale.
connects: This work builds on longstanding AI safety research that has emphasized the importance of pre-deployment testing and risk assessment for increasingly capable models. It extends earlier frameworks for identifying dual-use risks and potential harmful applications by documenting how these concerns are now driving real policy and technical decisions at major AI laboratories. The report connects to the broader literature on AI governance and institutional approaches to safety, as well as to technical work on robustness, adversarial defenses, and training methodologies that aim to make systems more aligned and controllable. It reflects the maturation of safety practices from theoretical discussions into concrete operational procedures across the industry.
enables: This assessment of current safeguarding practices provides a baseline for evaluating whether technical and institutional measures are scaling adequately with AI capabilities. By documenting which approaches are being adopted, which remain nascent, and where governance frameworks are emerging, it enables researchers to identify gaps in risk management coverage and prioritize future work. The report supports more rigorous analysis of whether existing safeguards are sufficient for increasingly powerful general-purpose systems, informs policy makers about industry practices, and establishes reference points for tracking whether safety innovations are being adopted industry-wide or remaining concentrated among leading developers.
